title: 10 best herbal teas for chills

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Purple coneflower tea contains compounds like triterpenoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause illness and contribute to chills.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and saponins present in purple coneflower tea may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as fever, headache, and body aches.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may exacerbate symptoms of illness like chills.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, teas helps with chills because of its unique properties. This herbal remedy contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid that have been shown to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. As a result, Echinacea purpurea teas can help to alleviate chills by boosting the body's natural defenses against infection and promoting a sense of warmth and comfort. This natural approach can be especially beneficial during cold and flu season.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline Alkaloids (Isoquinoline Glycosides): These compounds, including echinacoside and echinulin, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help alleviate chills by reducing the body's inflammatory response to infections.

Caffeic Acid Phenethyl Ester (CAPE): A phenolic compound, CAPE has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help combat chills by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by infections.

Cichoric Acid: A caffeic acid derivative, cichoric acid has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate chills by reducing inflammation, inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms, and neutralizing free radicals.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used because they contain high concentrations of active compounds, including alkylamides and glycosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Leaves: They are used because they are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are thought to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate symptoms of chills.

Flowers: They are used because they contain a higher concentration of volatile oils and other compounds that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and reduce fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 ounces of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from a healthy plant at dawn.
Dry the harvested plant material at 95-105 degrees fahrenheit for 1-2 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and add honey to taste to sweeten the echinacea purpurea infusion.
Store the herbal tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba tea contains antioxidants that can help combat oxidative stress and inflammation, which may contribute to the sensation of chilliness.
Vasoactive compounds: The tea contains vasodilators like ginkgolides and bilobalide, which can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, potentially helping to warm up the body.
Antipyretic properties: Some studies suggest that Ginkgo biloba has antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties, which may help alleviate the sensation of chilliness and fever.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with chills because of its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's flavonoids and terpenoids help increase blood flow to the skin, promoting warmth and relaxation. This improves circulation and reduces the sensation of coldness. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's properties may help reduce the severity and frequency of chills, providing relief for those experiencing cold intolerance. As a natural remedy, Ginkgo biloba tea offers a soothing and calming solution to help alleviate chills.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help combat chills by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to extremities, thus alleviating cold sensations.

Terpene lactones (mainly bilobalide and ginkgolide): These terpene lactones help alleviate chills by improving oxygenation of tissues, reducing oxidative stress, and enhancing blood flow to peripheral areas.

Ginkgolides (mainly ginkgolide B and C): These ginkgolides aid in reducing chills by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), a chemical involved in inflammation, thus potentially reducing cold sensations and improving circulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Extracts from Ginkgo biloba leaves are commonly used to make teas due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help alleviate chills.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used to make teas because they are a good source of ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help combat chills.
Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are used to make teas due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are still green and have maximum flavonoid content.
Rinse the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves with cool water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and dry them in a single layer at 80 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep 2 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the ginkgo biloba tea and let it cool before serving as a hot or iced beverage.

remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elder tea contains antiviral compounds that help combat viruses that cause colds and flu, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms like chills.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in elder tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce body aches, fever, and other uncomfortable symptoms associated with chills.

Expectorant properties: Elder tea has been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues like congestion, coughs, and colds, by helping to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs and airways, alleviating discomfort caused by chills.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, teas helps with chills because of its high concentration of antiviral and antihistamine properties. The plant's leaves and flowers contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of cold and flu symptoms, including chills. By supporting the body's natural defense mechanisms, elder tea helps to alleviate chills and promote overall well-being. Its soothing and relaxing effects also make it an ideal remedy for relieving body aches and fatigue associated with illness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of chills by alleviating underlying inflammatory responses in the body.
Phenolic acids (such as ferulic acid): These compounds exhibit antioxidant properties, which help to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, ultimately leading to a decrease in chills.

Volatile oils (such as limonene and pinene): These terpenes have decongestant and expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory congestion and promote the clearance of mucus, thereby reducing the sensation of chills.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of antiviral and antimicrobial compounds, such as sambunigrin, which help to combat cold and flu symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antiviral and antiseptic properties, which aid in reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues.
Buds: The buds are used for their potential to stimulate the immune system and help reduce inflammation, making them effective in fighting off colds and flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in mid to late summer when they are in full bloom.
Clean and dry the harvested sambucus nigra flowers and leaves thoroughly for 2 days.
Combine 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to infuse.
Strain and drink the tea immediately to reap its potential health benefits within 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: A natural decongestant that helps to ease congestion and relieve coughs, which can be a common symptom of chills.
Menthol: A natural analgesic that provides rapid relief from headaches, fever, and body aches associated with chills, by numbing the skin and mucous membranes.

Volatile oils: Containing menthone and isomenthone, these oils help to calm the digestive system, reducing nausea and stomach cramps that often occur when someone has chills, which can be a result of gastrointestinal upset or flu-like symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with chills because of its natural properties that aid in relieving cold and flu symptoms. The menthol content in peppermint has a cooling effect on the body, which helps to reduce fever and alleviate shivering. Additionally, the tea's decongestant properties help to clear nasal passages and sinuses, allowing the body to breathe more easily and recover from illness. This natural remedy promotes overall well-being and can provide quick relief from chills.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol acts as a natural analgesic and antipyretic, helping to reduce body temperature and relieve chills by blocking pain receptors and dilating blood vessels.
Catechins: Catechins are a type of phenolic compound found in peppermint tea that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the body's response to infection and alleviate chills.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound found in peppermint tea that can help reduce fever and alleviate chills by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part for teas due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which are responsible for the cooling and decongestant properties.
Stems: Stems are often used in combination with leaves, as they contain similar compounds that aid in relieving chills and promoting relaxation.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their delicate flavor and to add a touch of sweetness to teas, while also providing some of the same medicinal benefits as the leaves and stems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and stems from the garden.
Chop the harvested leaves and stems into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Steep 1 tablespoon of chopped mentha x piperita in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy the refreshing flavor and aroma.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lavender tea's antioxidants help to combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of chills and other cold-like symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender tea can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion, fever, and body aches associated with chills.
Anxiolytic properties: Lavender's calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for chills, and promote relaxation and restful sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, teas helps with chills because of its unique combination of properties. The tea's high linalool and linalyl acetate content has a calming effect on the body, reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The anti-inflammatory properties of the tea also help to soothe the body, reducing fever and alleviating cold symptoms. Additionally, the tea's ability to regulate body temperature and improve circulation helps to warm the body, making it an effective remedy for chills.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as an antipyretic, helping to reduce fever and alleviate chills by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene also has antipyretic properties, which contribute to its ability to reduce fever and alleviate chills by modulating the body's thermoregulation.
Luteolin: This phenolic compound exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to mitigate the underlying inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to chills and fever.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavandula angustifolia flowers are commonly used to make teas for chills due to their calming and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia contain antioxidants and other compounds that help to reduce fever and alleviate cold and flu symptoms.
Stems: Lavandula angustifolia stems also contain essential oils and other compounds that promote relaxation and reduce inflammation, making them a popular ingredient in teas for chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1/4 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers from a trusted source.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey or sugar to sweeten the tea to taste if desired.
Drink the tea immediately while still warm and enjoy its calming effects for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce body aches, joint pain, and fever, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating chill symptoms.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties in ginger tea help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughs associated with chills.

Natural antihistamine and decongestant: Ginger's antihistamine and decongestant effects help to reduce nasal congestion, sinus pressure, and alleviate symptoms of a cold or flu, which often accompany chills.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, teas helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and warming effects on the body. The active compound, gingerol, stimulates blood circulation, which can help to increase the body's temperature and reduce the sensation of coldness. Additionally, ginger's antimicrobial properties may help to fight off underlying infections that can cause chills. Drinking ginger tea can provide relief and comfort, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Its soothing properties can also promote relaxation and reduce discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These gingerols help with chills by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds, thereby alleviating cold and flu symptoms.
Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly 6-gingerol, have shown analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that may help relieve chills and fever associated with colds and flu.

Curcuminoids (specifically, Gingerol's curcumin-like properties): These compounds exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help combat the underlying causes of chills, such as colds, flu, and other infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote sweating, which can help to relieve chills and fever.
Buds: Have a higher concentration of volatile oils than mature rhizomes, providing a potent and warming effect when used in teas.
remedy_recipe: Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and warm water before handling the rhizomes.
Peel and chop 5-10 grams of fresh or 2-3 teaspoons of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes.
Combine the chopped rhizomes with 250-500 ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has cooled slightly to a comfortable temperature.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.

remedy_properties: Antipyretic Properties: Yarrow tea has been shown to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, making it an effective natural remedy for treating colds and flu. This property helps to cool down the body and reduce inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The bioactive compounds present in yarrow tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe sore throats, ease congestion, and reduce coughing fits that often accompany chills.

Antiviral and Antimicrobial Properties: Yarrow tea contains antiviral and antimicrobial agents that can help to combat the underlying infections causing chills, such as the common cold and flu viruses. This property helps to boost the immune system and fight off infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, teas helps with chills because of its natural antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, including flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, help to reduce body temperature and alleviate cold symptoms. Yarrow's ability to stimulate sweating also aids in removing toxins and excess fluids from the body, promoting a sense of warmth and comfort. This makes Achillea millefolium tea a popular natural remedy for relieving chills and fever.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds, particularly rosmarinic acid, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate chills by reducing body temperature and alleviating underlying inflammation.

Flavonoids (especially apigenin and luteolin): These plant-derived compounds have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to lower body temperature and alleviate chills associated with fever or infection.

Essential oils (including camphor and borneol): The vapor from these essential oils can help to stimulate the body's thermoregulatory centers, increasing circulation and promoting a feeling of warmth, which can help to counteract chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever and alleviate chills.
Flowers: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and calm the body during illness.
Stems: Utilized for their antimicrobial properties, which can aid in fighting off infections that cause chills and fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in a quantity of 1-2 teaspoons per cup of water.
Steep the achillea millefolium in boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release the herbal properties and flavors.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve to remove the achillea millefolium solids and any remaining particles.
Store the cooled achillea millefolium tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.
Serve the achillea millefolium tea chilled or at room temperature in a quantity of 1-2 cups per serving.

remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea contains compounds like eucalyptol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses and bacteria, helping to combat the underlying cause of chills.

Expectorant properties: The tea's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing the feeling of chilliness that often accompanies congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin, which may help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of chills and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, teas helps with chills because it contains eucalyptol, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory compound. This compound helps to relieve congestion and ease cold and flu symptoms, reducing the severity of chills. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus teas have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which help to combat the underlying infection causing the chills. remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: It acts as a decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to relieve chills by reducing the thickness of mucus and alleviating respiratory tract congestion.
Catechin: As a flavonoid phenolic compound, it has antioxidant properties that help combat the underlying viral or bacterial infection causing chills, thereby reducing the severity of the symptoms.
α-Terpineol: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help alleviate chills by reducing inflammation and the replication of pathogens in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a compound known for its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems contain a higher concentration of eucalyptol compared to the leaves and are often used to make teas that help relieve respiratory issues.
Barks: The barks are also used to make teas due to their ability to reduce fever and relieve congestion, making them a popular choice for cold and flu remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Rinse the harvested leaves with clean water to remove dirt and impurities from the plant.
Steep the leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the essential oils.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve to separate the solid leaves from the liquid.
Serve the eucalyptus globulus tea hot or iced with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice added to taste.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and ease congestion, which can alleviate symptoms such as fever and chills.

Soother for mucous membranes: Slippery elm's demulcent properties coat and soothe irritated mucous membranes in the throat, respiratory tract, and digestive system, helping to calm inflammation and reduce discomfort caused by chills.

Antioxidant and immune-stimulant: The antioxidants present in slippery elm tea help protect against oxidative stress and support the immune system, which can aid in fighting off infections that cause chills and fever.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, teas helps with chills because of its unique properties. The inner bark of the Ulmus rubra tree is rich in mucilages, which are soothing compounds that can help reduce inflammation and calm the body. When consumed as a tea, these mucilages can help regulate body temperature, alleviate chills, and promote overall well-being. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from cold and flu symptoms, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to health.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and modulate the body's response to temperature fluctuations, thereby alleviating chills.
Triterpenoids (such as ursolic acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe the body and reduce the sensation of coldness associated with chills.
Flavonoids (such as quercetin): These compounds have antipyretic (fever-reducing) and anti-inflammatory properties that help to regulate body temperature and alleviate symptoms of chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas for chills due to their fever-reducing and antipyretic properties.
Barks: Used to make teas for chills due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate discomfort and reduce fever.
Roots: Used to make teas for chills due to their warming and stimulating effects, which help to increase circulation and reduce fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried ulmus rubra bark and 1l of boiling water to make tea.
Steep the ulmus rubra bark in the boiling water for 5-10 minutes to infuse flavor.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove solids from liquid.
Discard the solids and store the tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator.
Serve the tea chilled or at room temperature after 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme tea's antibacterial properties help to combat bacterial infections, which can cause chills, by inhibiting the growth and replication of bacteria.

Decongestant properties: The decongestant compounds in thyme tea, such as thymol, help to relieve congestion and reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as fever, headaches, and fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, teas helps with chills because of its natural antiviral and antibacterial properties. Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid helps to reduce chills by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent that reduces the body's inflammatory response and alleviates symptoms associated with chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are often used to make teas for chills due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used to make teas for chills because they contain essential oils with decongestant and expectorant properties, which help to relieve cold and flu symptoms.
Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are sometimes used to make teas for chills as they contain volatile oils with antimicrobial properties, which aid in fighting off infections and reducing fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers from a reputable herbal supplier.
Boil one cup of fresh water in a teapot or kettle for 5 minutes.
Steep the dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Serve the thymus vulgaris tea immediately and enjoy after the brew has cooled slightly to lukewarm temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with chills because of its natural properties that promote warmth and comfort. The herbs in rosemary tea, such as camphor and borneol, have a warming effect on the body, helping to increase blood circulation and reduce muscle tension. This can help to alleviate chills and shivers caused by cold or flu, leaving you feeling warm and cozy inside. Additionally, rosemary's antimicrobial properties may help to combat infection, further contributing to its chill-reducing benefits.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Rosemary contains anti-inflammatory compounds like camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate chill symptoms such as muscle aches and pains.

Decongestant properties: Rosemary's volatile oils, particularly camphor, have natural decongestant properties that can help ease congestion and sinus pressure, common symptoms of chills caused by cold or flu.

Antimicrobial activity: Rosemary's antimicrobial compounds may help combat the underlying infection or bacteria that causes chills by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in combating chill-inducing fever.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds contain essential oils and flavonoids that help to warm the body and relieve cold-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat the water in a saucepan until it reaches a rolling boil then remove from heat immediately.
Add the measured rosemary leaves to the boiling water and let steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking within thirty minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass teas helps with chills because of its natural antipyretic properties, which effectively reduce fever and alleviate shivering. The tea's citral content works to break down and eliminate toxins from the body, helping to restore balance and calm the nervous system. Additionally, lemongrass has a soothing effect on the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and relax when feeling under the weather. Decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass tea's active compounds, such as citronella, help to reduce congestion and inflammation in the body, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms of chills like fever and aches.

Antipyretic properties: The flavonoids present in lemongrass tea have been shown to exhibit antipyretic (fever-reducing) effects, helping to lower body temperature and alleviate the discomfort associated with chills.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried cymbopogon citratus leaves in a clean glass container for further processing.
Boil one cup of water and let it simmer for five minutes before adding the herb.
Add two teaspoons of the dried cymbopogon citratus leaves to the boiling water and steep.
Allow the tea to steep for seven to ten minutes before straining and serving.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and enjoy while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with chills because of its unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and anti-inflammatory properties. Barks: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce fever and body aches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and dry them properly according to recommended guidelines for optimal potency.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried calendula flowers and add it to a teapot or infuser.
Heat filtered water to boiling point and pour it over the calendula flowers, allowing steeping time of 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves and discard solids, then transfer the liquid to a cup or carafe for immediate consumption.
Enjoy the warm tea within 30 minutes of preparation to help alleviate chills and promote relaxation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with chills because of its natural ability to warm the body from within. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in sage tea help to stimulate blood flow and increase circulation, which can help to raise the body's internal temperature and alleviate feelings of chilliness. Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain tannins and flavonoids that can aid in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or store-bought.
Rinse one tablespoon of dried salvia officinalis leaves with boiling water to remove impurities.
Steep the rinsed herbs in one cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release bioactive compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard solids before adding one teaspoon of honey or sweetener of your choice.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature of 70-80°f within 30 seconds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with chills because it contains compounds that have natural warming properties. The glycyrrhizin in licorice root has been shown to increase blood flow and warm the body, helping to alleviate shivering and chilliness. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of licorice tea can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are often linked with feelings of chilliness. By drinking a warm cup of herbal licorice tea, individuals may experience a sense of comfort and relief from chills, allowing them to feel more relaxed and at ease.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and fever, which can alleviate chills by targeting the underlying causes of the body's temperature regulation.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by viral infections that may be contributing to chills, promoting a faster recovery.
Isomaltose: Has antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial or viral infections that may be causing chills, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce fever and alleviate chill symptoms.
Barks: Contain triterpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing body aches associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and place it in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a kettle or pot and pour it over the dried root in the tea infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired strength of flavor.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a spoon or tea strainer and discard the solids.
Serve the tea hot, adding honey or lemon to taste if desired, and drink immediately to help alleviate chills.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with chills because of its natural diuretic properties that stimulate urine production, helping to flush out toxins and excess fluids from the body. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also help to soothe and calm the body's tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with chills. Additionally, dandelion tea has been shown to increase sweating, which can help to release built-up heat and toxins, further alleviating chill symptoms. Overall, dandelion tea provides a natural and effective way to combat chills and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to chills by reducing inflammation and promoting overall bodily warmth.
Diuretic properties: Dandelion's diuretic effects can help increase urine production, which can help rid the body of excess heat and toxins that may be contributing to chills.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of chills by promoting warmth and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are rich in bitter compounds that can help reduce fever and alleviate cold symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the dandelion plant contain anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe aches and pains associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale root per cup of boiling water.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the dried taraxacum officinale root in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it to help alleviate chills and reduce fever.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with chills because its soothing properties can calm the body's natural response to cold or flu, reducing fever and discomfort. The flavonoids present in chamomile tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which help to relax muscles and ease shivering fits. Additionally, chamomile's gentle sedative qualities promote a sense of relaxation, allowing the body to conserve energy and recover from illness more efficiently. As a result, sipping on warm chamomile tea can bring a sense of comfort and relief from chills.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Chamomile tea contains apigenin, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation and soothes irritated tissues, helping to alleviate chills caused by underlying infections or inflammation.
Antispasmodic properties: The flavonoids and terpenes in chamomile tea help relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can help calm shivering and other symptoms associated with chills.

Sedative effects: Chamomile tea's calming and soothing properties can help promote a sense of relaxation and calmness, reducing anxiety and stress that may exacerbate chilliness or worsen sleep disruptions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing chill symptoms.
Flowers: Containing apigenin, an flavonoid that helps reduce fever and alleviate chill-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers measuring one teaspoon per cup.
Boil fresh water in a kettle or on the stove for five to seven minutes.
Steep the dried chamomilla flowers in boiling water for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug.
Drink the warm tea immediately and repeat as needed up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort teas helps with chills because of its unique properties that promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and increase body temperature. remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Mugwort tea helps to reduce fever and alleviate chills by lowering body temperature and relieving discomfort caused by high fevers.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in mugwort tea, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of chills and flu-like conditions.

Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: Mugwort's antiviral and antimicrobial properties help to combat viral and bacterial infections that can cause chills, fever, and other cold-like symptoms by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and reducing their ability to replicate.
remedy_parts: Roots: Mugwort roots are used in teas to help alleviate fever and sweating associated with chills, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Mugwort leaves are used to calm the body and reduce shivering caused by chills, as they contain a soothing compound called thymol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of artemisia vulgaris, ensuring proper identification and cleanliness for use.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or low-temperature oven drying methods for 24 hours.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water, steeping for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding solids, and let it cool slightly before consumption.
Drink three cups of tea throughout the day, spaced evenly apart, as needed to alleviate chills.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover teas helps with chills because it is rich in isoflavones, which have been shown to help regulate body temperature. Additionally, red clover has natural antispasmodic properties that can help relieve muscle aches and pains often associated with chills. Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate body aches, fever, and discomfort associated with chills, promoting a sense of well-being and relaxation.

Vasodilatory effects: Red clover's vasodilatory compounds can help widen blood vessels, improving circulation and increasing the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the body's cells, which can help combat chill-induced symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Red clover roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and body aches associated with chills.
Flowers: The flowers of red clover contain isoflavones, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue that come with chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried red clover flowers according to local regulations and guidelines.
Measure one teaspoon of dried flowers or three teaspoons of fresh flowers per eight ounce cup water.
Steep the flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release beneficial compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and particles.
Drink the warm tea immediately, allowing it to cool slightly before consuming while experiencing chills.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with chills because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the body. The mucilage in marshmallow root coats the throat and chest, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with chills. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to relax muscles and ease congestion, allowing for easier breathing and a sense of warmth and comfort. As the body fights off an infection or illness, marshmallow teas provides gentle support and relief from the chills that often accompany it.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Marshmallow tea helps to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion associated with chills.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The mucilage in marshmallow root reduces inflammation and soothes irritated tissues, helping to alleviate discomfort and pain caused by chills.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Marshmallow tea has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the development of chills, reducing their severity and duration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated respiratory tracts, making it effective against chills.
Stems: Containing a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried marshmallow root and 1 cup boiling water.
Steep the marshmallow root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink immediately.
Prepare a fresh batch every 1-2 hours as needed throughout the day.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chills that are treated with herbal teas are fever, colds, and influenza. Herbal teas such as peppermint, ginger, and eucalyptus can help alleviate symptoms of these ailments by warming the body and easing congestion. Other herbs like yarrow, elderflower, and willow bark can also be used to treat similar symptoms, promoting sweating and reducing inflammation to help bring down fever and relieve discomfort.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for chills are a natural and soothing remedy that help alleviate the symptoms of chills by promoting relaxation, reducing fever, and boosting immunity. The best herbal teas for chills include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Eucalyptus globulus, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Sambucus nigra, Thymus vulgaris, Achillea millefolium, Rosmarinus officinalis, and Cinnamomum verum. If left untreated, chills can lead to missed workdays, disrupted sleep patterns, and a weakened immune system, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with chills is a blend of Ginger, Echinacea, and Peppermint. Ginger helps to warm the body and alleviate nausea, while Echinacea boosts the immune system to fight off underlying infections. Peppermint adds a soothing and calming effect, helping to reduce fever and promote relaxation. This trio of teas works together to provide natural relief from chills, promoting a speedy recovery and overall well-being. Regular consumption can help to prevent recurring chills.
